display.purpose: Hero headline and landing-page CTAs only. Never used inside the product app.

headline.purpose: Section titles on public pages; the Monera wordmark in sidebar and mobile header.

title.purpose: Card titles and page section headers inside the product app. Always DM Sans — serif stays outside the app.

body.purpose: Narrative paragraphs, insight copy, descriptions. Max 65–75ch line length.

label.purpose: Button labels, form labels, navigation items, UI controls.

mono.purpose: Every financial amount, percentage, budget figure, and transaction value. font-variant-numeric: tabular-nums always.

motion.name: loading-bar
motion.purpose: Header loading indicator bar — animates x from -100% to 100% on data fetch.

motion.name: state-transition
motion.purpose: Default CSS transition for hover/focus state changes on buttons, nav items, inputs.

breakpoints.name: md
breakpoints.note: Tailwind md — desktop/mobile switch: sidebar vs bottom bar, hidden nav labels

components.name: Primary Button
components.description: Single highest-priority action per screen. Anchor Navy — used sparingly.
components.css: .ds-btn-primary { display: inline-flex; align-items: center; justify-content: center; gap: 8px; background: var(--primary); color: var(--primary-foreground); height: 40px; padding: 8px 16px; border: none; border-radius: 8px; font-family: 'DM Sans', system-ui, sans-serif; font-size: 0.875rem; font-weight: 500; line-height: 1.4; cursor: pointer; white-space: nowrap; transition: background 0.15s ease-out; } .ds-btn-primary:hover { background: #162a47; } .ds-btn-primary:focus-visible { outline: none; box-shadow: 0 0 0 2px var(--background), 0 0 0 4px var(--ring); } .ds-btn-primary:disabled { opacity: 0.5; cursor: not-allowed; pointer-events: none; }

components.name: Outline Button
components.description: Secondary action alongside a primary button.
components.css: .ds-btn-outline { display: inline-flex; align-items: center; justify-content: center; gap: 8px; background: var(--card); color: var(--foreground); height: 40px; padding: 8px 16px; border: 1px solid var(--border); border-radius: 8px; font-family: 'DM Sans', system-ui, sans-serif; font-size: 0.875rem; font-weight: 500; cursor: pointer; white-space: nowrap; transition: background 0.15s ease-out; } .ds-btn-outline:hover { background: var(--secondary); } .ds-btn-outline:focus-visible { outline: none; box-shadow: 0 0 0 2px var(--background), 0 0 0 4px var(--ring); } .ds-btn-outline:disabled { opacity: 0.5; cursor: not-allowed; }

components.name: Card
components.description: Primary container surface. White on off-white. No shadow, border only.
components.html:
Safe to Spend

What you can still spend before payday

€342.50
components.css: .ds-card { background: var(--card); border: 1px solid var(--border); border-radius: 12px; overflow: hidden; } .ds-card-header { padding: 24px 24px 0; display: flex; flex-direction: column; gap: 4px; } .ds-card-title { font-family: 'DM Sans', system-ui, sans-serif; font-size: 1rem; font-weight: 600; line-height: 1.4; color: var(--foreground); margin: 0; } .ds-card-desc { font-family: 'DM Sans', system-ui, sans-serif; font-size: 0.875rem; line-height: 1.4; color: var(--muted-foreground); margin: 0; } .ds-card-content { padding: 12px 24px 24px; } .ds-amount { font-family: 'DM Mono', ui-monospace, monospace; font-size: 1.5rem; font-weight: 500; color: var(--foreground); font-variant-numeric: tabular-nums; }

components.name: Text Input
components.description: Standard text field. Focus ring is the only focus signal — border does not change.
components.css: .ds-field { display: flex; flex-direction: column; gap: 6px; } .ds-label { font-family: 'DM Sans', system-ui, sans-serif; font-size: 0.875rem; font-weight: 500; color: var(--foreground); } .ds-input { height: 40px; width: 100%; background: var(--card); border: 1px solid var(--input); border-radius: 8px; padding: 8px 12px; font-family: 'DM Sans', system-ui, sans-serif; font-size: 0.875rem; color: var(--foreground); outline: none; transition: box-shadow 0.15s ease-out; } .ds-input::placeholder { color: var(--muted-foreground); } .ds-input:focus { box-shadow: 0 0 0 2px var(--background), 0 0 0 4px var(--ring); } .ds-input:disabled { opacity: 0.5; cursor: not-allowed; }

components.name: Sidebar Nav
components.description: Desktop sidebar navigation. Active state is a whisper of Anchor Navy bg — sufficient to orient without dominating.
components.html:
MoneraPersonal Finance
DashboardTransactionsInsights
components.css: .ds-sidebar { display: flex; flex-direction: column; width: 224px; background: var(--card); border-right: 1px solid var(--border); padding-bottom: 12px; } .ds-sidebar-brand { padding: 24px 20px 16px; } .ds-wordmark { display: block; font-family: 'DM Serif Display', Georgia, serif; font-size: 1.5rem; font-weight: 400; color: var(--foreground); line-height: 1.2; } .ds-wordmark-sub { display: block; font-family: 'DM Sans', system-ui, sans-serif; font-size: 0.75rem; color: var(--muted-foreground); margin-top: 2px; } .ds-sidebar-nav { display: flex; flex-direction: column; gap: 2px; padding: 4px 12px; } .ds-nav-item { display: flex; align-items: center; gap: 12px; padding: 8px 12px; border-radius: 8px; font-family: 'DM Sans', system-ui, sans-serif; font-size: 0.875rem; font-weight: 400; color: var(--muted-foreground); text-decoration: none; transition: background 0.1s ease-out, color 0.1s ease-out; } .ds-nav-item:hover { background: var(--secondary); color: var(--foreground); } .ds-nav-item--active { background: color-mix(in oklch, var(--primary) 8%, transparent); color: var(--primary); font-weight: 500; } .ds-nav-item--active svg { stroke-width: 2.5; }

components.name: Budget Progress Bar
components.description: Budget status bar with semantic fill colors. Emerald = OK, Amber = Warning, Red = Over. Never use these colors decoratively.
components.html:
Needs€820 / €1,100
74%€280 left
components.css: .ds-budget-bar { display: flex; flex-direction: column; gap: 6px; } .ds-budget-bar__header { display: flex; justify-content: space-between; align-items: center; } .ds-budget-bar__label { font-family: 'DM Sans', system-ui, sans-serif; font-size: 0.875rem; font-weight: 500; color: var(--foreground); } .ds-budget-bar__amounts { font-family: 'DM Mono', ui-monospace, monospace; font-size: 0.75rem; font-variant-numeric: tabular-nums; } .ds-budget-bar__amounts--ok { color: #059669; } .ds-budget-bar__amounts--warning { color: #d97706; } .ds-budget-bar__amounts--over { color: #dc2626; } .ds-budget-bar__track { height: 6px; width: 100%; background: var(--secondary); border-radius: 9999px; overflow: hidden; } .ds-budget-bar__fill { height: 100%; border-radius: 9999px; transition: width 0.3s ease-out; } .ds-budget-bar__fill--ok { background: #10b981; } .ds-budget-bar__fill--warning { background: #f59e0b; } .ds-budget-bar__fill--over { background: #dc2626; } .ds-budget-bar__footer { display: flex; justify-content: space-between; font-family: 'DM Mono', ui-monospace, monospace; font-size: 0.75rem; color: var(--muted-foreground); font-variant-numeric: tabular-nums; } .ds-budget-remaining--ok { color: #059669; } .ds-budget-remaining--warning { color: #d97706; } .ds-budget-remaining--over { color: #dc2626; }

components.name: Badge
components.description: Pill badge for classification and status. Primary = Anchor Navy. Secondary = warm surface.
components.html: SubscriptionFood & DrinkRecurring
components.css: .ds-badges { display: flex; gap: 8px; flex-wrap: wrap; } .ds-badge { display: inline-flex; align-items: center; border-radius: 9999px; padding: 2px 10px; font-family: 'DM Sans', system-ui, sans-serif; font-size: 0.75rem; font-weight: 600; background: var(--primary); color: var(--primary-foreground); border: 1px solid transparent; } .ds-badge--secondary { background: var(--secondary); color: var(--foreground); border-color: transparent; } .ds-badge--outline { background: transparent; color: var(--foreground); border-color: var(--border); }

narrative.overview:
Every screen in Monera should feel like a well-kept private notebook — precise entries, deliberate whitespace, nothing decorative that doesn't earn its place. The interface serves the user's financial record, not the other way around. Data is the protagonist; chrome and ornamentation are the supporting cast.

The design walks a careful line: warm enough to feel like a personal tool (not an enterprise dashboard), calibrated enough to earn trust with money (not a lifestyle app). Confidence is expressed through restraint — Anchor Navy appears only where it counts: calls to action, active navigation states, focus rings, and progress fills. Everywhere else, the palette recedes and lets numbers speak.

This system explicitly rejects the utilitarian, spreadsheet-y aesthetic of traditional budgeting tools — the kind where the design communicates "tool" before "product." It also rejects badge-heavy privacy marketing: security claims expressed as shield icons on every surface read as anxiety, not assurance. Privacy here is architectural; the copy explains the mechanism once, and the interface simply executes it, cleanly and without fanfare.
keyCharacteristics: Flat surfaces; tonal depth through surface stacking, not shadows
One primary color (Anchor Navy) on ≤10% of any given screen
DM Serif Display reserved for brand moments; DM Sans carries the product
DM Mono for every financial figure without exception
Card White on Off-White: the tonal separation does the work of shadows
All borders and surfaces stay in one warm tonal family

rules.name: The One Anchor Rule
rules.body: Anchor Navy is the only saturated color used on neutral UI. Budget status colors (emerald / amber / destructive) are semantic signals, not accent colors. If a new component wants more color, the answer is Anchor Navy — there is no secondary accent.

rules.name: The Tonal Stack Rule
rules.body: Surfaces are layered in one direction: Off-White (page) → Card White (surface) → Warm Surface (hover/muted/track). Never invert this order. Never place a Warm Surface card on a Card White background.

rules.name: The Serif Boundary Rule
rules.body: DM Serif Display appears only in two contexts: the Monera wordmark and section headings on public-facing pages. Inside the product app, all headings use DM Sans.

rules.name: The Mono Lock Rule
rules.body: Every financial amount, percentage, budget figure, and transaction value uses DM Mono with font-variant-numeric: tabular-nums. No exceptions. Mixing DM Sans and DM Mono in the same row of numeric data is prohibited.

rules.name: The Flat-By-Default Rule
rules.body: Surfaces are flat at rest. No box-shadow on cards, inputs, drawers, or popovers. If a component needs more visual presence, use a slightly darker border or a tonal background shift — not a shadow.

rules.name: The Blur Exception
rules.body: backdrop-filter: blur is reserved for one surface: the sticky app header. Blurred glass cards or any other decorative application of this property are prohibited.

dos: Do use DM Mono with font-variant-numeric: tabular-nums for every financial figure — amounts, percentages, budget ratios, transaction values.
Do place Anchor Navy on ≤10% of any screen. One primary CTA, one active nav state, one focus ring per view.
Do follow the tonal stack: Off-White → Card White → Warm Surface.
Do explain privacy mechanically in copy: 'your own Google Drive', 'drive.file scope — it can only see files it created.'
Do use payday-period framing throughout the product UI. Never 'this month'.
Do apply text-wrap: balance to h1–h3 headings.
Do include :focus-visible with a visible 2px Anchor Navy ring on every interactive element.
Do use emerald / amber / destructive-red exclusively for budget status (OK / Warning / Over).
donts: Don't use DM Serif Display inside the product app for anything except the Monera wordmark.
Don't add box-shadow to cards, inputs, drawers, or containers.
Don't apply backdrop-filter: blur outside the sticky app header.
Don't use emerald, amber, or destructive-red decoratively — their meaning is fixed.
Don't let the design look like YNAB or classic budgeting spreadsheet apps.
Don't badge-stamp privacy claims. No shield icons as decorative surface elements.
Don't use calendar-month language inside the product. 'This pay period' or 'since [payday]' is correct.
Don't nest a card inside a card.
Don't use background-clip: text with a gradient.
Don't use border-left or border-right wider than 1px as a colored accent stripe.
Don't build identical card grids (same-size card, icon + heading + text, repeated).
